Since 1999

Lexan Law &
Corporate Services

We are a full-service law firm that specialises in litigation and arbitration as well as transactions and regulatory compliance — practising before all High Courts of Pakistan.

Contact Us Our Lawyers

We are a full-service law firm that specialises in litigation and arbitration as well as corporate transactions and regulatory compliance. Founded in 1999 by Malik Zahid Hussain, Senior Advocate High Court, the firm practises before all High Courts of Pakistan and has served as trusted legal counsel to financial institutions, corporations and individuals for over two decades, with 14 cases reported in Pakistan's authoritative legal reporters.

Practice Areas

01

Corporate Law & Advisory

Company formation, governance, SECP compliance, JV agreements, M&A and international commercial agreements.

02

Banking & Financial Litigation

Recovery suits, dishonoured instruments, SBP enforcement, Banking Court representation and loan restructuring.

03

Criminal Defence & NAB

NAB trials, anti-corruption proceedings, criminal bails, acquittals and constitutional petitions nationwide.

04

Civil & Property Disputes

Possession suits, specific performance, land acquisition, recovery of damages and injunctions.

05

Constitutional Petitions

Judicial review, writs, fundamental rights enforcement and public interest litigation.

06

Family & Rent Law

Matrimonial disputes, custody, maintenance, inheritance, khula and rent tribunal proceedings.

07

Intellectual Property

Trademark, copyright and patent registration and infringement proceedings before IP Tribunals.

08

Arbitration & ADR

Domestic and international arbitration, mediation and negotiated settlements.

News & Briefings

PLD 2019
Lahore 43
Constitutional Law · Featured
Hameed Akhtar Vs. Federation of Pakistan — Challenging the Validity of NAO 1999 Before the Lahore High Court

In a landmark constitutional petition, Malik Zahid Hussain argued before the Lahore High Court's Division Bench challenging the constitutionality of provisions of the National Accountability Ordinance 1999, raising critical questions of fundamental rights and separation of powers.

Lahore High CourtPLD 2019 Lahore 438 min read

About

Malik Zahid Hussain is the Founder and Managing Partner of Lexan Law & Corporate Services. Enrolled as Advocate with the Punjab Bar Council on 29 September 1999 and licensed before the High Courts of Pakistan on 19 November 2002, he has over 25 years of continuous practice spanning Constitutional Law, Corporate Governance, Banking Litigation, NAB Defence, Insurance, Intellectual Property and Public Interest Litigation before the Lahore, Sindh, Peshawar, Multan and Rawalpindi High Courts, as well as NAB Courts, Banking Courts, Insurance Tribunals, IP Tribunals and regulatory bodies including SECP, PSX and SBP.

In 2002 he established the firm in the name and style of "Law & Lawyers" — subsequently rebranded as Lexan Law & Corporate Services — with branch offices at Lahore, Karachi and Okara. He advises local and international clients on licensing, regulatory matters, international financial agreements, Pakistan securities regulation, capital markets law, winding up, insolvency and private client law.

About

Aatif Parvez Chaudhry is an Advocate enrolled since 2001 with High Court licence from 2003. He was the first lawyer to lodge a Consumer Case in Punjab, and has over 23 years of legal practice in civil, property, banking, consumer, revenue, cooperative and criminal law. PBC Reg. No. 28267.

Office: No. 102 Auon Tower, Ground Floor, Behind Punjab Bar Council, Lahore. Tel: 042-32362157 · Cell: 0300-4240772 · aatifchaudhry@hotmail.com

F.Sc Pre-Medical — 1993–1995

2001 to date — Practising Civil (Property), Banking, Consumer, Revenue, Cooperative and Criminal Law

First lawyer to lodge a Consumer Case in Punjab

Executive Member — GSCHS Lahore
Associated with Meezan Bank for court cases (two years)
Active member of Consumer Council — held consumer awareness seminars with Provincial Consumer Council
Three-day workshop on Practice and Procedure of Banking
